title: Ensure that the --authorization-mode argument is not set to AlwaysAllow

input_row.title: Ensure that the --authorization-mode argument is not set to AlwaysAllow
input_row.description: Do not allow all requests. Enable explicit authorization.
input_row.rationale: Kubelets, by default, allow all authenticated requests (even anonymous ones) without needing explicit authorization checks from the apiserver. You should restrict this behavior and only allow explicitly authorized requests.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on each node: ps -ef | grep kubelet If the --authorization-mode argument is present check that it is not set to AlwaysAllow . If it is not present check that there is a Kubelet config file specified by --config , and that file sets authorization: mode to something other than AlwaysAllow . It is also possible to review the running configuration of a Kubelet via the /configz endpoint on the Kubelet API port (typically 10250/TCP ). Accessing these with appropriate credentials will provide details of the Kubelet's configuration.
input_row.remediation: If using a Kubelet config file, edit the file to set authorization: mode to Webhook . If using executable arguments, edit the kubelet service file /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d/10-kubeadm.conf on each worker node and set the below parameter in KUBELET_AUTHZ_ARGS variable. --authorization-mode=Webhook Based on your system, restart the kubelet service. For example: 188 | P a g e systemctl daemon-reload systemctl restart kubelet.service
input_row.default_value: By default, --authorization-mode argument is set to AlwaysAllow .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_kubelet_authorization_mode_not_alwaysallow

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to check the Kubelet configuration.
- The command `kubectl get nodes -o json` can be used to retrieve node information, including Kubelet configuration.
- Specifically, check the `spec.kubelet` section for the `authorizationMode` field.
- If the Kubelet is configured using a config file, check the file specified by the `--config` argument for the `authorization.mode` setting.
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: `authorization.mode` is set to `Webhook` or another secure mode.
  - Fail: `authorization.mode` is set to `AlwaysAllow` or is not set at all.
- Example: Use `kubectl get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].status.nodeInfo}'` to verify the Kubelet's configuration and ensure the `authorization.mode` is not set to `AlwaysAllow`.
